frameworks.name: reward-complexity trade-off
frameworks.one_line: Optimal policy balances expected reward against action-policy complexity

frameworks.name: information-theoretic policy complexity
frameworks.one_line: Policy complexity formalized as an information-theoretic cost on actions

frameworks.name: perseveration
frameworks.one_line: Choice repetition recast as a consequence of minimizing policy complexity

core_question: What is the computational origin of choice perseveration independent of reward history?
key_assumptions: Agents pursue two goals: reward maximization and policy-complexity minimization
Policy complexity admits an information-theoretic formalization
Optimal trade-off between reward and complexity governs choice behavior
manipulations: Re-analysis of two existing choice data sets
Parameter estimation under the trade-off model
Model comparison against alternative accounts of perseveration
key_findings: Humans operate close to the optimal reward-complexity trade-off
Optimizing the trade-off analytically yields perseveration
Perseveration matches the theoretically predicted functional form
Model comparison supports the trade-off account quantitatively
interpretations: Perseveration is not a bias but a signature of bounded-rational policy compression
A single information-theoretic principle links reward seeking and choice repetition
